author: codex
scope: validator/rule-data triage only for next relation endpoint bucket after next4; no code, canonical, materialized, wiki, manifest, override, journal, or LOG edits

recommendation.summary: Do not widen the implemented_by endpoint validator to accept treatment_component subjects yet. The next largest post-next4/post-batch023 bucket is six treatment_component -> entity implemented_by rows, but the examples are mixed: three Blue Lighting rows have an obvious same-source project carrier, one Pelham Bay row is a treatment/entity implementation detail without a specific project record, one Help Point row is closer to LIRR field-visit/program-support context than implementation, and the ABLE row reflects program-vs-treatment modeling. This should be curated as data cleanup rather than encoded as a broad validator rule.
recommendation.recommended_action: Prepare a small retire-and-replace cleanup batch for the six malformed implemented_by treatment_component -> entity relations. Use project -> entity implemented_by replacements where the source already has a same-source project carrier; otherwise use a reviewed looser relation such as related_to or add/attach a proper project/program carrier in a separate cleanup lane if evidence supports it.

do_not_patch_validator_yet.reason: The current schema and agent instructions keep agency_role implemented_by scoped to project -> entity, while treatment components attach upward through has_treatment. Allowing treatment_component -> entity would hide endpoint/modeling problems and admit weak rows where the text says only field-visit support or broad program ownership.

checkpoint_context.given_checkpoint: 88 unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape issues before next4; has_metric claim -> metric_claim rule patched but not full-validated.

current_code_observed.observed_acceptance: relationEndpointShapeIssue("has_metric", "claim", "metric_claim") is accepted in current code/tests.

excluded_from_this_triage.reason: next4 has_metric claim -> metric_claim validator patch

targeted_inventory_after_exclusions.method: Read-only bun --eval over readCanonicalRecords() and relationEndpointShapeIssue(); excluded relation023 original record ids; did not run CLI validate.

implemented_by_rule.current_rejection_message: Relation kind implemented_by expects project -> entity, got treatment_component -> entity.

related_rules_and_docs_checked.finding: Endpoint table lists implemented_by as project -> entity and has_treatment as project/corridor -> treatment_component.
Treatment relation pass says treatment observations should attach to a project or corridor via has_treatment; it does not make treatments first-class agency-role carriers.
Current RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES.implemented_by admits only project -> entity.

subject.description: Blue LED lighting installed at LIRR stations; NYCT and Metro-North pilots this year

evidence.text: LIRR installed at 26 stations; NYCT and Metro-North pilots this year

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: Same source already has project_meeting-doc-108036-blue-lighting with local_observation_id project_meeting_doc_108036_blue_lighting; replace with implemented_by project -> entity rather than broadening the validator.

evidence.text: LIRR installed at 26 stations; NYCT and Metro-North pilots this year

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: Use the same Blue Lighting project carrier if preserving Metro-North pilot implementation; the text supports an agency role but the subject should be the project/program record.

evidence.text: LIRR installed at 26 stations; NYCT and Metro-North pilots this year

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: Likely same project -> entity replacement as the other Blue Lighting rows.

subject.description: Bus contra-flow lane on Pelham Bay being painted red by NYC DOT, to enable rerouting Bx12 SBS to Co-op City

evidence.text: The Bus network redesigns are a major project intended to directly address these concerns.
We are also excited to announce additional bus lane improvement on Pelham Bay where our partners at NYC DOT are painting the street red.

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: The source supports NYC DOT involvement with a bus-lane treatment. It does not provide a precise same-source project record for this Pelham Bay lane, so this row needs curated cleanup, not a blanket implemented_by validator widening.

subject.description: Help Point units installed at Deer Park Station with site assessments conducted for upcoming installations at Wyandanch Station and Farmingdale Station on the Ronkonkoma Branch.

evidence.text: LIRR's Program Support team proudly hosted members of the LIRR ADA Task Force and the Commuter Council for a field visit...
The day began at Deer Park Station, where Help Points have already been

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: This is weaker as implemented_by: the cited block says Program Support hosted a field visit, not that LIRR implemented the Help Point treatment. Retire/replace with a weaker contextual relation or defer for source-specific cleanup.

subject.description: ABLE deploys automated cameras on buses which ticket vehicles illegally operating in bus lanes.

evidence.text: Our Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) camera program continues to improve bus speeds by 5%...
Building on the success of bus lanes is NYCT's ABLE camera program.
ABLE also is a win for safety: ABLE corridors have seen a 20% reduction in collisions on average.

exact_issue_examples.cleanup_read: The source calls ABLE a camera program. Existing canonical project records for ABLE/ACE exist, but not as a same-source project in meeting_doc_127471. This is a modeling cleanup candidate, not a validator-shape patch candidate.

simulated_impact.why_cleanup_has_same_endpoint_count_impa: All six rows are in one shape bucket; either retiring/replacing them or admitting the shape would remove six endpoint-shape issues. Cleanup is preferred because it preserves the validator as a signal for treatment/project modeling.

candidate_cleanup_plan.recommended_batch_name: relation cleanup batch024 or next available relation cleanup lane

rows.preferred_replacement: curated cleanup; likely related_to treatment_pelham_bay_bus_contraflow_lane -> entity_nyc_dot_nov2022 unless a precise same-source project carrier is added/reviewed
curated cleanup; source supports LIRR Program Support context more clearly than implemented_by, so prefer related_to or defer if no useful relation remains
curated cleanup; either attach ABLE to a proper project/program carrier or use related_to treatment_component_able_program -> entity_meeting_doc_127471_nyct if preserving only source-backed context

candidate_cleanup_plan.dry_run_status: Not dry-run through createSubmissionEntry in this triage artifact; this note recommends the bucket and replacement strategy only.

deferred_competing_buckets.reason: Still mixed claim scheduling/context semantics; prior cleanup precedent handled similar claim timeline context as data cleanup, and it should not be bundled with implemented_by treatment rows.
Metric/event date context may need separate modeling review; admitting metric_claim timeline subjects would be broader than the inspected evidence supports.
Prior notes classify these agency/service entity pairs as mixed and often self-link-like; do not broaden agency-role predicates to all entity pairs.
Claim publication may be plausible for some rows, but current rows need their own publisher/claim semantics review.

commands_run.outcome: Confirmed repo root and located relation/validation/review files; output was broad.
Confirmed very dirty shared worktree; treated all existing changes as external and did not revert or overwrite.
Listed prior review artifacts and confirmed next4/batch023 context files exist.

Read batch023 prep and extracted the five has_timeline_event treatment_component -> event original relation record ids to exclude.
Confirmed current has_metric claim subject patch/test is present and implemented_by remains project -> entity.

commands_run.outcome: Found canonical ABLE/ACE project records but no same-source meeting_doc_127471 project carrier for the offending ABLE row; supports curated data cleanup rather than a narrow rule patch.

commands_run.command: bun --eval 'relationEndpointShapeIssue checks for implemented_by treatment_component->entity, implemented_by project->entity, related_to treatment_component->entity, has_treatment project->treatment_component'
commands_run.outcome: Confirmed current validator rejects implemented_by treatment_component->entity, accepts implemented_by project->entity, accepts related_to because it has no endpoint-shape rule, and accepts has_treatment project->treatment_component.
